title: Supporting the goals and ideals of Korean American Day.
plain_summary: This resolution supports Korean American Day, which happens every January 13th. This day celebrates when the first Korean people came to America in 1903, which was 122 years ago. The resolution recognizes all the good things Korean Americans have done for the country in business, government, the military, and culture. The House of Representatives wants all Americans to celebrate this day and think about how Korean Americans have helped make America better.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Resolution Supporting the goals and ideals of Korean American Day
sections.text: Korean Americans have made important contributions to American life in many areas including politics, business, volunteering, arts, and education. On January thirteenth, nineteen oh three, one hundred and two brave Korean immigrants came to the United States. This was the start of the first big wave of Korean people moving to America. These first Korean immigrants faced huge social and economic challenges and had trouble with the English language when they came to America, which they saw as the land of opportunity. As they worked toward the American dream, Korean immigrants first worked as farm workers, wage workers, and railroad workers across the United States. The first generation of Korean immigrants built new homes in a new country through strength, hard work, and great sacrifice. This became the foundation for their children and future generations of Korean Americans. The year two thousand and three marked one hundred years since Korean immigration began, which was an important milestone. The House of Representatives passed House Resolution four hundred eighty seven to honor Korean American Day during the one hundred ninth Congress. The Senate passed Senate Resolution two hundred eighty three to honor Korean American Day during the one hundred ninth Congress. Korean Americans, like other groups of immigrants who came to the United States before them looking for a better life and more opportunities, have succeeded in their new country because of their strong work habits, family connections, and community spirit. Korean Americans have made important contributions to the economic strength of the United States and the world marketplace. Korean Americans have made history by winning elections across the country at local, state, and federal levels of government. Korean Americans have strengthened businesses, nonprofit organizations, government, technology, medicine, sports, arts and entertainment, journalism, churches, schools, and many other parts of American life. Korean Americans have built and strengthened the friendship between the United States and the Republic of Korea, helping to create peace on the Korean Peninsula. Korean Americans have made huge contributions to the military strength of the United States and served with honor in the Armed Forces during World War One, World War Two, the Vietnam War, the Korean War, and other military conflicts around the world. The Centennial Committees of Korean Immigration and Korean Americans have chosen January thirteenth of each year as Korean American Day to remember the first step of the long and successful journey of Korean Americans in the United States. Therefore, the House of Representatives supports the goals and ideals of Korean American Day. The House urges all Americans to observe Korean American Day so they can better appreciate the valuable contributions Korean Americans have made to the United States. The House honors and recognizes the one hundred twenty second anniversary of the arrival of the first Korean immigrants to the United States.
